full_prompt: Design a single-part monolithic main wing spar (center carry-through structure) for a VTOL fixed-wing UAS per the NIST First Responder UAS Endurance Challenge (HeroX). Mission: carry a 10 lb (4.5 kg) comms-relay payload continuously for >= 90 min at 30 m/s cruise; VTOL takeoff and landing; ignition-kill capability required. Vehicle is a quad-tiltrotor tail-sitter with a 3.0 m wingspan and 20 kg MTOW (15.5 kg empty + 4.5 kg payload). This prompt is for the wing center-spar: a single carbon-composite part that carries wing-root bending moment and houses the wing-to-fuselage bolted joint. Geometric constraints: spar length 1200 mm (fuselage-width + wing-root joint both sides); spar cross-section hollow rectangular 80 mm x 40 mm with 4 mm wall; 8x M6 tapped wing-attach holes on each end in a 60 mm x 30 mm pattern; 4x M8 fuselage-attach holes at center in a 100 mm x 40 mm pattern. Material: T700S carbon fiber / epoxy unidirectional prepreg [0/+45/-45/90]s quasi-isotropic layup, E1 (fiber) = 135 GPa, E2 = 10 GPa, G12 = 5 GPa, X_tension = 2050 MPa, X_compression = 1470 MPa, Y_tension = 70 MPa, Y_compression = 170 MPa, density = 1600 kg/m^3. Load cases: (R1) 3.8 g pull-up maneuver (FAA Part 23 utility-class limit): 20 kg x 3.8 x 9.81 = 745 N lift; distributed over 3.0 m span produces wing-root bending moment of approximately 745 N x 0.75 m = 559 N*m; evaluate spar with Tsai-Wu failure index <= 1.0 / 1.5 = 0.667 (ultimate factor 1.5 per FAR 23.303). (R2) -1.5 g negative-g push-over: wing-root moment reversed, magnitude 220 N*m, Tsai-Wu index <= 0.667. (R3) 6 g hard landing: VTOL hard set-down 20 kg x 6 g = 1177 N at fuselage attach through spar, Tsai-Wu index <= 0.9 (yield factor). (R4) Tip strike during VTOL transition: 200 N horizontal at one wingtip, root moment 200 x 1.5 = 300 N*m, Tsai-Wu index <= 0.667. (R5) Spar mass <= 450 g. (R6) First modal frequency spar hard-mounted at fuselage attach with wing-tip masses lumped at ends >= 15 Hz (above UAS propeller 1P at approximately 5 Hz with 3x margin). (R7) Linear buckling eigenvalue of spar under compressive bending flange under LC1 >= 2.0. Primary verification: structural FEA (linear static x4 with progressive composite failure) + modal + linear buckling. Vibration secondary. Thermal, fluid, radiation NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 + Parasolid spar, layup diagram, and a report listing spar mass in g, Tsai-Wu index per case, first modal frequency in Hz, first buckling load factor.
short_prompt:
Design the single-part carbon-composite main wing center carry-through spar of a quad-tiltrotor tail-sitter VTOL UAS for the NIST First Responder UAS Endurance Challenge, carrying a 4.5 kg (10 lb) comms-relay payload >= 90 min at 30 m/s cruise. Wingspan 3.0 m, MTOW 20 kg.

Envelope: spar 1200 mm long, hollow rectangular 80 x 40 mm with 4 mm wall; 8 x M6 wing-attach bolts on 60 x 30 mm patterns at each end; 4 x M8 fuselage-attach bolts at center on a 100 x 40 mm pattern; T700S quasi-isotropic [0/+45/-45/90]s layup.

Factor regime: FAR 23.303 ultimate FoS 1.5 on limit load for composite, Tsai-Wu failure index <= 1.0 / 1.5 = 0.667 on limit cases.

Load cases.
- +3.8 g pull-up (FAR 23 utility): 745 N lift, wing-root bending ~559 N-m.
- -1.5 g push-over: reversed moment ~220 N-m.
- 6 g hard VTOL landing: 1177 N at the fuselage attach.
- Tip strike during VTOL transition: 200 N horizontal at wingtip, root moment 300 N-m.
- Linear buckling eigenvalue on compressed flange under LC1 >= 2.0.
- Spar-hard-mounted first modal frequency >= 15 Hz.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Unidirectional CFRP quasi-isotropic layup requires Tsai-Wu progressive failure, not isotropic von Mises.

prompt.title: NIST first responder UAS wing spar
prompt.brief: T700S quasi-iso monolithic center wing spar, 20 kg MTOW VTOL tiltrotor, 3.8 g + VTOL hard-landing + tip-strike.

geometric_constraints.wing_attach_bolts: 8 x M6 on 60 x 30 pattern each end
geometric_constraints.fuselage_bolts: 4 x M8 on 100 x 40 pattern at center

boundary_conditions.mounting: fuselage-attach bolts fully fixed for structural and buckling; lumped wing-tip masses for modal
boundary_conditions.loads: root bending moments or tip forces

analysis_types: linear static x4 with composite failure

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Aerodynamic loads reduced to wing-root moments.

eval_coverage: NIST endurance mission wing-root structural budget
